systems.name: Converra CMS
systems.summary: Converra is a full-stack digital signage CMS consisting of a Next.js web dashboard, an Express REST API backend, and an Electron desktop player app — enabling centralized content scheduling, playlist management, and real-time multi-screen remote control.
systems.description: Converra is the most complex system built at MJ Solution Indonesia — a production-grade digital signage platform with three distinct layers. The Next.js frontend serves both as an admin dashboard and a browser-based player interface, with screen connection flow and auth pages. The Express backend handles REST API operations, JWT authentication, file uploads up to 100MB, and dual real-time strategies — native WebSocket in development and Pusher Channels in production. The Electron desktop player (mjs-cms-desktop) runs on physical display devices, authenticates via token, receives remote commands in real time, supports idle video and content playback, and ships with an auto-update mechanism across 4 published releases. Storage is handled by local filesystem in development and Cloudinary CDN in production. The entire system is deployed across Vercel (frontend), Railway (backend), and Aiven PostgreSQL (database).
systems.problem: Managing content across multiple digital display locations required manually updating each player device on-site — a slow, error-prone workflow that became impossible to scale as the number of screens and clients grew. There was no way to monitor screen health, schedule content in advance, or push updates remotely without physical access to each device.
systems.solution: A three-part centralized platform where operators manage all content, playlists, and screens from a single web dashboard. The Electron player app runs silently on display hardware, authenticates with a token, and receives real-time commands via Pusher or WebSocket — enabling full remote control of every registered screen without any physical intervention.
highlights: Three-part architecture: Next.js web dashboard, Express backend, and Electron desktop player
Electron player app with 4 published releases (latest v1.1.1 — March 2026)
Dual real-time strategy: native WebSocket (development) and Pusher Channels (production)
Dual storage strategy: local filesystem (development) and Cloudinary CDN (production)
Token-based screen authentication for secure player registration
Remote playback control: play, stop, screen on/off from dashboard
Auto-update mechanism on Electron player
JWT authentication
Prisma ORM with separate migration flows for development, testing, and production
Deployed across Hostinger, Railway, and Aiven PostgreSQL
features: Content management: images, videos, and web pages
Playlist builder with scheduling
Multi-screen management dashboard
Remote playback control (play, stop, screen on/off)
Real-time screen status monitoring (online/offline)
Token-based screen authentication and connection flow
Browser-based player interface (Next.js)
Electron desktop player with idle video and auto-update
JWT-based admin authentication
File upload with automatic format validation (up to 100MB)
Cloudinary CDN media storage with local filesystem fallback
Pusher Channels pub/sub for production real-time features
Native WebSocket server for local development
Prisma Studio for database inspection
Separate migration commands per environment (dev/testing/production)
Next.js middleware for route protection
Custom React hooks and API service clients
Audit log for content and publish actions

systems.name: MJS Event Registration
systems.summary: A system event registration and check-in management system with real-time QR code verification, admin dashboard, and audit logging — built with Next.js and PostgreSQL.
systems.description: MJS Event Registration is a system for managing event attendee registration and on-site check-in. Attendees register via a public form, receive a unique QR code upon successful submission, and are verified at the venue through a QR scanner page. Admins access a protected dashboard with registration management, analytics charts, and full audit logs. The system uses Supabase PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM, Upstash Redis for rate limiting, and NextAuth for session-based authentication — suitable for internal company events as well as client-facing event management needs.
systems.problem: Manual event registration and check-in processes are slow, error-prone, and difficult to audit — especially for mid-to-large scale corporate events where real-time attendee verification and data accuracy are critical.
systems.solution: A web-based registration system where attendees self-register and instantly receive a QR code. On event day, staff use the built-in QR scanner to verify attendance in real time. Admins monitor registrations, view analytics, and access audit logs from a protected dashboard — replacing spreadsheets and manual processes entirely.
highlights: Real-time QR code generation per registrant on successful submission
Built-in QR scanner page for on-site check-in verification
Protected admin dashboard with registration management and analytics
Full audit log for all check-in and registration activity
Rate limiting via Upstash Redis to prevent form abuse
Session-based authentication with NextAuth
Supabase PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM and connection pooling via PgBouncer
Input validation with Zod schemas
Deployed on Vercel with automatic CI/CD from main branch
features: Public registration form with validation
QR code generation and success page post-registration
QR scanner page for real-time check-in
Admin login with NextAuth session management
Dashboard home with analytics charts
Registrations table with management controls
Audit log viewer
API routes: register, check-in, QR image generation, CRUD operations
Rate limiting on registration endpoint
Database seeding for admin user setup

systems.name: MJS LED Configurator
systems.focus: configuration led to help sales team
systems.summary: A web-based LED & LCD display configurator built for MJ Solution's sales team and clients — enabling real-time technical calculations, room placement simulation, processor recommendations, and PDF proposal export.
systems.description: MJS LED Configurator is an internal web tool developed to streamline the LED & LCD display quotation process at MJ Solution Indonesia. Users input basic parameters such as room dimensions, desired screen size, pixel pitch, and installation type (indoor/outdoor). The system automatically calculates screen resolution, module count, real physical dimensions, remaining wall space, viewing distance, power consumption, and recommends compatible LED processors — all following industry-standard formulas. Clients can also upload a photo of their actual location to preview how the LED screen would look in their space, and export the full technical result as a professional PDF proposal.
systems.problem: The LED display quotation process at MJ Solution was slow and manual — sales had to gather extensive technical data before pricing, clients had no visual reference of the final result, and on-site surveys were required just to measure room dimensions before any calculation could begin in Excel.
systems.solution: A browser-based configurator that lets sales staff and clients independently configure LED displays by entering room and screen parameters. The system handles all technical calculations automatically, simulates screen placement in the client's uploaded room photo, recommends compatible processors, and generates a ready-to-send PDF proposal — eliminating manual Excel work and reducing the need for on-site surveys.
highlights: Eliminates manual Excel-based LED calculations
Clients can self-configure LED displays without technical background
Real-time calculation of resolution, module count, real size, and remaining wall space
Room photo upload with LED screen overlay for realistic preview
Automatic processor (sending box/controller) recommendation based on final resolution
PDF export with technical specs, simulation image, and device recommendations
Industry-standard formulas: resolution = module size ÷ pixel pitch, real size = resolution × total modules
Supports indoor and outdoor installation types
Responsive design: works on laptop, tablet, and smartphone
features: Flexible input: room dimensions, desired screen size, pixel pitch, installation type

Room placement simulation with visual preview

interactive.name: MJS Memory Card
interactive.summary: A two-stage memory card matching game built for MJ Solution event booths — featuring avatar selection, timed stages, sound effects, and a real-time Supabase leaderboard.
interactive.description: MJS Memory Card is a browser-based memory card game designed for event booths and exhibitions. Players enter their name, pick an avatar, then race through two timed stages matching product image pairs. Stage 1 runs for 60 seconds and Stage 2 for 45 seconds — with a 5-second countdown overlay between stages. Scores are ranked by total completion time and saved to a Supabase leaderboard. The UI supports two layout modes: desktop landscape and portrait signage display, making it ready for both kiosk and standard screens.
interactive.problem: Event booths often rely on passive displays or printed materials that fail to engage visitors. There was no interactive experience that could draw crowds, encourage participation, and organically introduce MJ Solution's products.
interactive.solution: An interactive game that disguises product exposure as entertainment — card images are MJ Solution products, and the competitive leaderboard drives repeat plays. The dual layout mode means the same app runs on both standard laptops and portrait digital signage screens at the booth.
highlights: Two-stage timed gameplay (60s + 45s) with 5-second countdown overlay between stages
6 randomized product image cards per stage for replayability
Real-time leaderboard ranked by fastest total completion time (Supabase PostgreSQL)
Podium display for top 3 with avatar, name, and time
Sound effects for flip, match, wrong, and win via Howler.js AudioManager singleton
Dual layout mode: desktop (landscape 4×3 grid) and signage (portrait 3×4 grid)
Zustand global state management for player, stage, and settings
3D flip card animation via Framer Motion
Avatar selection with 6 options on registration screen
features: Player registration: name input and avatar selection
Stage countdown overlay (5 seconds) before each stage
Memory card board with 3D flip animation
Match detection with combo tracking
Per-stage timer with visual countdown
Win modal showing Stage 1 time, Stage 2 time, and total time
Game over modal with retry, home, and leaderboard options
Leaderboard page with podium (rank 1-3) and rank list (rank 4-10)
Sound toggle (on/off) from game header
Portrait/landscape layout auto-detection via useLayoutVariant hook
Client-side only — no SSR, all DB access via Supabase JS SDK

interactive.name: MJS Scream Challenge
interactive.focus: real-time audio input, event booth experience
interactive.summary: A microphone-based scream challenge game for MJ Solution × Arch ID event booths — players scream into the mic for 60 seconds, accumulate score via dB intensity and combo multiplier, and winners unlock a Spin the Wheel prize.
interactive.description: MJS Scream Challenge is an interactive event booth game where players scream or blow into their device microphone for 60 seconds. Sound intensity is captured in real time via the Web Audio API and converted to a score using dB thresholds, rate multipliers, and a live combo system. Two difficulty levels are available: Normal and Hard, each with different dB thresholds and scoring rates. Players who reach a score of 300+ are classified as winners and receive a link to the Spin the Wheel prize page. All scores are saved to a Supabase leaderboard with podium and rank display. Built for the MJ Solution Indonesia × Arch ID brand event.
interactive.problem: Standard event booth activities are often passive and forgettable. There was a need for a high-energy, instantly understandable interactive experience that could draw a crowd, create shareable moments, and integrate with an existing prize mechanic.
interactive.solution: A mic-based game with a clear win condition, live visual feedback via a power meter, and a combo multiplier that rewards sustained effort. The 60-second format is short enough for quick turnover at the booth while the leaderboard and Spin the Wheel integration create incentive for competitive replay.
highlights: Real-time microphone dB tracking via Web Audio API and requestAnimationFrame
Combo multiplier system that scales with accumulated score (x1 to x5)
Two difficulty levels: Normal (dB threshold -70 to -30 dBFS) and Hard (-60 to -10 dBFS)
Win condition: score ≥ 300 unlocks Spin the Wheel prize link
60-second game timer with live score accumulation display
Power meter visualization with 4 tiers (Weak → Max) using balloon animation
Supabase leaderboard with top-10 ranking by score
Phase-based state machine: register → instruction → countdown → playing → result
3-2-1-SCREAM countdown animation before game starts
Dual layout support: mobile default and desktop/signage via useLayoutVariant
Background video on game screen
features: Player registration: name, avatar selection, difficulty level
Instruction screen with 3-step game guide
Animated 3-2-1-SCREAM countdown screen

interactive.name: MJS Photobooth
interactive.focus: media capture, cloud storage, event booth experience
interactive.summary: An interactive photobooth web app for events — featuring webcam capture, layout and frame customization, photo filters, and result delivery via email, WhatsApp request, or QR code download.
interactive.description: MJS Photobooth is a browser-based photobooth application designed for MJ Solution event installations. Users go through a guided flow: welcome screen, layout selection, photo capture via webcam, preview with frame overlay composited using html2canvas, and final delivery via QR code, email, or WhatsApp request. The composited photo is uploaded to a Supabase Storage bucket named after the event, and a QR code is generated from the public URL for instant download. Email delivery uses Nodemailer via a Next.js API route. WhatsApp requests are logged to a Supabase database table and managed through a separate admin dashboard page.
interactive.problem: Physical photo prints at events require expensive hardware and on-site operators. There was no digital alternative that could deliver a polished, branded photo experience and get results directly into visitors' hands without manual intervention.
interactive.solution: A fully browser-based photobooth with a guided multi-screen flow that captures, composites, and delivers branded photos digitally — via QR code scan, email, or WhatsApp — with zero need for physical printing hardware. An admin dashboard lets the team manage and track WhatsApp delivery requests.
highlights: Multi-screen guided flow: Welcome → Layout → Photo Capture → Preview → QR Delivery
Webcam photo capture with layout and frame selection
Final image compositing with frame overlay using html2canvas
QR code generation from Supabase Storage public URL for instant download
Email delivery with photo attachment via Nodemailer (Next.js API route)
WhatsApp request queue stored in Supabase DB with admin dashboard
Dynamic Supabase Storage bucket per event name
Centralized session state via React Context (PhotoboothProvider)
Material UI v6 themed component system
features: Welcome and event selection screen
Layout and frame customization
Live webcam capture with multiple shots
Photo preview with frame overlay compositing (html2canvas)

interactive.name: Happify
interactive.summary: Happify is a full-stack custom merchandise e-commerce platform — featuring an interactive canvas-based product designer, AI background removal, cross-device QR checkout, Midtrans QRIS payment, and automated email and WhatsApp order notifications.
interactive.description: Happify is a custom merchandise platform built by MJ Solution Indonesia, allowing customers to personalize products — T-shirts, socks, tote bags, and gift cards — through an interactive canvas designer. Users can upload photos with client-side AI background removal, browse Pixabay stock designs, and edit gift cards. Cart state persists in localStorage, with a cross-device QR checkout flow that lets kiosk users transfer their cart to a mobile device via Supabase-backed checkout sessions. Payment is handled via Midtrans Snap (QRIS only). Post-payment, a webhook triggers automated order confirmation emails via Resend (to customer and production team) and WhatsApp resi notifications via Fonnte. An admin dashboard handles order and shipping management.
interactive.problem: Ordering custom merchandise typically requires back-and-forth communication with a designer, manual calculations for shipping, and no real-time visibility into order status. For event or kiosk contexts, completing a purchase on a small screen or shared device adds further friction.
interactive.solution: A self-service customization platform where customers design their own products, calculate shipping in real time via Binderbyte API, and pay via QRIS — all in one flow. The cross-device QR checkout solves the kiosk-to-mobile handoff problem, while the Express backend and Midtrans webhook automate all post-payment notifications and order logging without manual intervention.
highlights: Interactive canvas-based product customizer (T-shirt, socks, tote bag, gift card)
Client-side AI background removal via @imgly/background-removal and MediaPipe
Pixabay stock design search with proxy API route
Cross-device QR checkout: kiosk → Supabase checkout_sessions → mobile prefill
Midtrans Snap integration (QRIS only, sandbox)
Automated post-payment email to customer and production team via Resend
WhatsApp resi notification via Fonnte API
Real-time shipping cost calculation and region selection via Binderbyte API
PDF order summary generation and temp file management
Admin dashboard for order and shipping management
Separate Express.js backend (port 3001) for payment and notification logic

interactive.name: MJS Product Showcase
interactive.focus: interactive product presentation for sales and marketing
interactive.summary: An interactive product showcase web app for MJ Solution Indonesia, built to replace static presentations with a dynamic, navigable product exploration experience for sales events and client meetings.
interactive.description: MJS Product Showcase is an interactive web-based presentation tool designed for MJ Solution's sales and marketing team. It serves as a digital catalog covering two main product categories — LED Display and LCD Display — featuring clean layouts, micro-interaction animations, and slide-in product detail sections. The project originated from a stakeholder meeting where static PowerPoint presentations were deemed insufficient for engaging product walkthroughs. Custom isometric illustrations were created in Adobe Illustrator to visually communicate product use-cases without heavy text, and the final product has been actively used in exhibitions, trade shows, and client meetings.
interactive.problem: Static PowerPoint presentations were inefficient for product demonstrations at events and client meetings — they lacked interactivity, required too much verbal explanation, and failed to give audiences a clear spatial understanding of how digital display products fit real environments.
interactive.solution: A web-based interactive prototype that lets sales and marketing teams navigate between product categories with smooth transitions and micro-interactions. Custom isometric illustrations visualize real-world use cases, while an intuitive user flow allows audiences to explore products independently — making presentations faster, more engaging, and more professional.
highlights: Initiated from a stakeholder meeting as a replacement for static PowerPoint decks
Covers two product categories: LED Display and LCD Display
Micro-interactions on every user action: page transitions, slide-in details, expandable sections
Custom isometric illustrations created in Adobe Illustrator for visual storytelling
Actively used in trade events, exhibitions, and client meetings
Received positive feedback from stakeholders and visitors
Reduced presentation time while increasing perceived brand professionalism
No technical background required to navigate — designed for any audience

landing_pages.name: VisionLAB
landing_pages.summary: Multi-page landing site for VisionLAB by MJ Solution Indonesia — a professional multimedia service center specializing in LED & LCD screen repair, maintenance, and on-site training.
landing_pages.description: VisionLAB is a multimedia service center under MJ Solution Indonesia, focused on repair, maintenance, and servicing of LED & LCD screens. The website serves as a full-featured service landing page with dedicated pages for each service, a partner brand showcase, maintenance process breakdown, tiered service packages, and an FAQ section — all driving visitors to WhatsApp-based consultation.
landing_pages.problem: As demand for digital displays rises, many LED and LCD screens degrade from poor or irregular maintenance, leaving businesses with underperforming or broken displays and no reliable professional service partner to restore them.
landing_pages.solution: VisionLAB provides end-to-end LED & LCD screen care — from complete hardware repair and calibration to scheduled maintenance contracts and on-site technician training — handled by certified technicians with experience across major international display brands.

landing_pages.name: Envision
landing_pages.summary: Landing page for Envision, a premium immersive experience company offering the 'Multiverse Engine' — a sensory platform combining micro-LED visuals, kinesthetic interaction, atmospheric audio, and spatial design.
landing_pages.description: Envision is a high-end immersive experience platform that goes beyond traditional display solutions. Their flagship product, the Multiverse Engine, blends micro-LED seamless displays, real-time 8K/16K rendering, motion, atmospheric-synthesizer audio, and sensory elements into a fully bespoke experience. The landing page positions Envision as an end-to-end partner for venues, corporations, and institutions looking to transform spaces into unforgettable visual destinations.
landing_pages.problem: In an era where audiences expect more than passive viewing, conventional display solutions fail to deliver truly immersive, emotionally engaging environments. Spaces lack the integration of visuals, motion, sound, and sensory elements needed to create lasting experiences.
landing_pages.solution: Envision's Multiverse Engine delivers a fully custom-built sensory platform — combining micro-LED displays, high-performance media servers, kinesthetic interaction, and atmospheric design — all engineered and installed end-to-end, from discovery and UX design to deployment and long-term support.
